summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a memorable 6/22/86 show with standout Fire On The Mountain, Box of Rain and Lovelight, though Jerry’s second‑set playing was uneven for some listeners.
review: The recording (soundboard) sounds solid overall with only minor drop‑outs that can be fixed, delivering a clear picture of the band’s late‑summer vibe. Fans rave about the mellow Fire On The Mountain opener, the long first set and a triumphant Box of Rain, while others note Jerry Garcia’s vocals were stellar but his guitar work slipped on parts of the second set.

ai_rating.rationale: Seven detailed reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise the recording; only one low‑star review points to performance flaws, giving a strong overall positive impression with solid technical quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Minor snap/drop‑out glitches noted (fixable in post), but generally clear and well‑balanced soundboard capture.

poor_songs: Fire On The Mountain (as noted by one reviewer for sloppy guitar)
show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, jam‑heavy first set followed by a slow, mellow Fire opener to the second set; double encore with U.S. Blues. Overall pacing praised for its relaxed Sunday‑afternoon feel, though one listener felt the second set lost momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as perfect and goose‑bump inducing; his solo on Fire was a highlight for many, but another reviewer called his guitar work dreadful on Fire and ragged later in the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Forgot lyrics during Box of Rain but recovered nicely; famously cleared his throat before the song, delighting the crowd.
band_member_comments.Bob: Delivered an improvised "Sweet Sweet Summertime" within Lovelight that many fans label as one of the sickest freak‑outs of ’86.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s background vocals and ragtime staccato were praised; some noted a lack of signature Lovelight licks before Bob’s summer rap.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s delayed fills on Lovelight were highlighted as “killer delays,” and the Drumz > Space > Truckin' sequence was called psychedelic and essential.
